composition.Programmer 1:
Fix and complete the backend code for the 'OnlineAuction' web application in main.py.
- Remove the enclosing triple quotes to make the Flask app and all route functions executable and importable.
- Ensure the Flask app instance named 'app' is properly defined and exposed at the module level.
- Implement routing for all frontend pages, ensuring each route corresponds to the frontend code.
- Handle file read and write operations starting from the first line of the file, using plain text without encryption.
- Support all nine pages  Dashboard, Auction Catalog, Auction Details, Place Bid, Bid History, Auction Categories, Winners, Trending Auctions, and Auction Status.
- Load and save data from/to the specified text files in the 'data' directory.
- Ensure the website is accessible via local port 5000, with the first page accessible at route '/'.
- Coordinate with Programmer 2 who will develop the frontend templates that interact with these backend routes.
composition.Programmer 2:
Develop the frontend code for the 'OnlineAuction' web application.
- Implement all frontend pages as HTML templates with embedded Python templating (e.g., Jinja2) to interact with backend routes.
- Ensure all URLs in the frontend code correspond to routes defined in the backend, not static HTML file names.
- Implement UI elements and page structures exactly as specified in the page design section, including all IDs and elements.
- Ensure navigation buttons link correctly to backend routes.
- Pages to implement  dashboard.html, catalog.html, auction_details.html, place_bid.html, bid_history.html, categories.html, winners.html, trending.html, status.html.
- Coordinate with Programmer 1 to ensure frontend templates correctly consume backend data and routes.
